figures.caption: FIG. 6. The KJΓΓ′J3-model. a The third N.N. x, y, z-bonds in dashed lines. b Magnetic moments of the zigzag order lying in the ac-plane with tilt angle α. c INS intensity at zero field. d-f INS intensities for field directions along a-, b-, c-axes. The discontinuity near Γ is due to that the INS intensity (15) does not tend to a constant limit at k→ 0.

FIG. 1. Phase diagram and INS intensities of the proximate KQSL in the KJΓ-model. a (upper panel) near the FM KQSL phase; b (lower panel) near the AFM KQSL phase. The phase diagrams are adapted schematically from Ref. [21]. The momentum path convention is shown in Fig. 2. The INS intensity is plotted in logarithmic scale introduced in Sec. IV B.

FIG. 2. Lattice and Brillouin zone (BZ) conventions. The unit cells are shown in black dashed lines with: a two sublattices (KQSL, FM, AFM); b four sublattices (zigzag, stripy); c six sublattices (120◦). The first and second BZs of the honeycomb lattice are shown in gray and black solid lines respectively. The BZs of the four- and six-sublattice unit cells are shown in grey dashed lines.

FIG. 3. INS intensities for the Kitaev-Heisenberg model inside the AFM phase at J > 0. The intensities are shown in logarithmic scale introduced in Sec. IV B which makes the magnon mode more visible. In a, c-d the off-set value c = 1 introduced below Eq. (15). In b we set c = 10−4 to highlight the low-intensity features, compared to a which focuses on the sharp gapless Goldstone mode. The apparent anisotropy of the Goldstone mode spin velocity in a-b is due to that the momentum path lengths M1 → K1, K1 → K′ are scaled differently.

FIG. 5. INS intensity near the transition points for the Kitaev-Heisenberg model with FM K. Due to proximity to the critical Jcr, both phases admit MF solutions. a in the stripy phase and b the QSL phase at J = 0.103|K|. c in the FM phase and d the QSL phase at J = −0.127|K|. The KQSL phase intensity is normalized to be the same as that in the corresponding ordered phase.
